zakatClaimEvidence: The Muslim Community Center Chicago explicitly mentions "Zakat Request" as one of its services. They provide an "Online Zakat Form" for applications and an email address (zakat@mccchicago.org) for inquiries. Their website also states, "Charity 'Zakah' One of the most important principles of Islam is that all things belong to God, and that wealth is therefore held by human beings in trust. The word zakat means both 'purification' and 'growth'. Our possessions are purified by setting aside a proportion for those in need, and like the pruning of plants, this cutting back balances and encourages new growth." Furthermore, they have a process for "zakat and/or sadaqa financial assistance" where applications are approved or denied at the discretion of The Muslim Community Center of Chicago. (Source: https://mccchicago.org/zakat-request/)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $10,751,849, a program expense ratio of 93.4%, and provides religious and educational services.
Financial metadata indicates the organization holds 23.6 months of working capital and a fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised.
The organization maintains active community governance through presidential elections and operates the MCC Academy.
The organization offers various giving methods to support its religious and cultural mission.
The Muslim Community Center is Zakat-eligible and maintains a formal process for Zakat requests and distribution.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and strong community alignment, though its impact score is tempered by a focus on capacity building over direct outcome data.
areas_for_improvement: Transparency regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics for its educational programs.
Enhancing third-party impact verification to supplement internal reporting.

dimension_explanations.alignment: MCC shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a Zakat-eligible institution with a clear policy for distributing funds to those in need [5]. Its long-standing track record in providing essential religious services and maintaining community governance through democratic elections results in a strong donor fit [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates strong financial health with a total revenue of $10,751,849 and a high program expense ratio of 93.4% [1]. While it maintains a robust 23.6 months of working capital, the impact score of 30/50 reflects a focus on capacity building and religious infrastructure where specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not publicly standardized [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Community Center (MCC) serves as a cornerstone of Chicago's Muslim community through comprehensive religious services and high-impact educational programming.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 93.4%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support community services.
Strong financial stability with 23.6 months of working capital available.
Highly efficient fundraising operations, spending only $0.03 to raise every $1.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Center (MCC) is a multi-faceted religious and educational institution dedicated to the teaching and recitation of the Holy Quran, daily congregational prayers, and funeral and marriage services [1][3]. The organization operates the MCC Academy and provides extensive children and adult education programs to foster community growth [1][3].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified financial efficiency and long-term operational success, but a lack of standardized outcome metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MCC creates impact by providing religious services and education that foster Islamic identity and community cohesion, though this pathway is not formally documented.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that educational and religious programs are effectively meeting the spiritual and academic needs of the community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Strong revenue growth and high reserves suggest capacity for expansion, though specific capital projects are not listed.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="5">93.4% of its expenses toward programs</cite> and maintains a lean <cite id="6">fundraising cost of only $0.03 per $1 raised</cite>.
With <cite id="1">over 55 years of continuous operation</cite> and <cite id="8">23.6 months of working capital</cite>, the organization shows high institutional maturity.
MCC distributed <cite id="7">over $714,000 in grants</cite> to 68 recipients, including <cite id="9">$75,000 to Medglobal Inc</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim Community Center's financial health is characterized by a high degree of liquidity and sustainable growth. The organization reported a <cite id="10">3-year revenue CAGR of 17.8%</cite>, growing from $7.1 million in 2021 to nearly $10 million by 2023. This growth is supported by a diversified revenue model where <cite id="11">$6,039,439 is generated through program services</cite>, such as school tuition, while <cite id="12">$2,861,538 comes from public contributions</cite>. Furthermore, the organization maintains a strong balance sheet with <cite id="13">total assets of $19,394,203</cite> and a healthy <cite id="8">working capital ratio of 23.6 months</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term strategic initiatives.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Center (MCC) is a foundational religious and educational institution that has served the Chicago metropolitan area since its <cite id="1">founding in 1969</cite>. Operating across multiple campuses in Chicago, Morton Grove, and Skokie, the organization provides a comprehensive suite of services including <cite id="2">daily and Friday congregational prayers</cite>, funeral services, and marriage ceremonies. A primary pillar of its mission is the <cite id="3">teaching and recitation of the Holy Quran</cite> alongside the operation of the MCC Academy, which offers full-time K-12 education. Financially, the organization demonstrates significant scale, reporting <cite id="4">total revenue of $9,945,910</cite> in the 2023 tax year. MCC maintains an <cite id="5">exceptional program expense ratio of 93.4%</cite>, indicating that its entire budget is directed toward its core religious and educational missions. This efficiency is further supported by a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than industry standards. Beyond its local services, MCC acts as a community hub for philanthropy, distributing <cite id="7">$714,141 in domestic grants</cite> to various humanitarian and community partners.
